title: penstemon fasciculatus
intro: Penstemon fasciculatus, also known as Rocky Mountain Penstemon or Beardtongue, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Penstemon genus. Native to the western United States and Canada, this plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The roots and leaves of Penstemon fasciculatus are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are said to provide relief from congestion, sore throats, and other respiratory problems.
